cases.title: Lockman v. Presbytery of Gulf Coast
cases.synopsis: Victor E. Lockman appealed the October 13, 1979 action of the Presbytery of Gulf Coast; the Presbytery of Gulf Coast also submitted a reference requesting General Assembly make final disposition under BCO 42-3. The Committee recommended both be found in order and referred to a Judicial Commission appointed by the Eighth General Assembly with authority to make final disposition.

cases.title: Mays v. Presbytery of New River
cases.synopsis: TE Rod Mays filed a complaint dated November 22, 1979 against the Presbytery of New River for its failure to deal with reported scandalous activity involving one of its ministers. The Committee recommended the complaint be found in order and a Judicial Commission be appointed to adjudicate the case at the General Assembly.

cases.title: Snapp and Hope v. Calvary Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Byron Snapp and TE James Hope

cases.synopsis: TE Byron Snapp and TE James Hope filed a complaint dated January 28, 1980 against Calvary Presbytery for improper use of the extraordinary clause (BCO 22-4) in the examination of a candidate for ordination at the January 24, 1980 presbytery meeting. The Committee recommended the complaint be found in order and a Judicial Commission be appointed to adjudicate the case at the General Assembly.

cases.title: Campbell et al. v. Presbytery of Mid-Atlantic

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE James H. Campbell, et al.

cases.synopsis: RE James H. Campbell and others filed a complaint dated February 6, 1980 against the Presbytery of Mid-Atlantic regarding its handling of the case of TE Robert Schoof. The Committee recommended the complaint be found in order, its lateness excused due to extenuating circumstances, and a Judicial Commission be appointed to adjudicate the case at the General Assembly.

cases.title: Bogue et al. v. Presbytery of the Ascension

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Carl W. Bogue, et al.

cases.synopsis: TE Carl W. Bogue and others filed a complaint dated March 3, 1980 against the Presbytery of the Ascension for approving a minister whose views on charismatic gifts appeared contrary to the Constitutional Standards, and for approving a call that did not comply with BCO 21-1. The Committee recommended the complaint be found in order and a Judicial Commission be appointed to adjudicate the case at the General Assembly.
